result:
All five functions are implemented in `problems.py` and pass a thorough set of checks covering every edge case the docstrings call out, plus a randomized brute-force cross-check.

- **`parse_duration`** — anchored regex (`([0-9]+)h`/`m`/`s`, each optional, in order) with a requirement that at least one component matched; anything else — non-strings, whitespace, reordered/duplicate/bare units, negatives, trailing garbage, even non-ASCII digits — raises `ValueError`.
- **`luhn_valid`** — strips spaces/hyphens, returns `False` for non-strings, empty results, or any non-ASCII-digit character, then runs the standard right-to-left double-every-second-digit checksum (`"0"` valid, `"1"`–`"9"` not).
- **`flatten`** — recursive, builds a fresh dict (input untouched), joins keys with `sep`, empty nested dicts contribute nothing, non-dict values (lists included) pass through as-is.
- **`top_k_frequent`** — `Counter` plus a sort key of `(-count, word)` for the alphabetical tie-break; returns `[]` for `k <= 0` and all distinct words when `k` is oversized.
- **`rolling_max`** — monotonic-deque O(n) sliding window; raises `ValueError` for `window <= 0`, returns `[]` when the window exceeds the input. 1M elements with window 5000 completes in ~0.37s, comfortably within a tight time budget.

Names, signatures, and docstrings are unchanged, only the standard library is used, and no commits were made.
